sections.paragraph:
adj., constr. טְהֹר sometimes טְהָר־ Job 17:9 Pro 22:11 [separated in Thes., see טְהֹר ], pure, specially-(a) clear, opp. to filthy (as to a garment), Zec 3:5.-(b) unmixed, unalloyed, e.g. as of gold, Exo 25:11, seq.; 28:36 .-(c) in a Levitical sense, as opposed to unclean, polluted, Levit. 13:17 hence applied to animals used in food, Gen 7:2 8:20 .-(d) in a moral sense, Psa 12:7 19:10 51:12 , לֵב טָהֹור “a pure heart;” Job 14:4.

Subst. purity, Pro 22:11.
